search_query: how a point mutation inactivates protein
combined_text: Why can one change in a nucleotide sequence of a gene cause a nonfunctional protein to be produced? Science & Mathematics Biology Why can one change in a nucleotide sequence of a gene cause a nonfunctional protein to be produced?1. The change can cause a stop codon to occur early in translation and the full length protein is not translated. 2. A substitution can result in one incorrect amino acid
